key_insight: Thermal hysteresis in NiTiCu is transferred into the band structure of a periodic thin-rod composite, creating temperature-dependent acoustic stop bands with different heating and cooling paths.
engineering_relevance: This provides a way to design tunable phononic filters or vibration isolators whose transmission can be switched or history-programmed by temperature and geometry.
method: Transfer-matrix analysis of 1D periodic rods with alternating NiTiCu and Parylene C segments
Compute Bloch band structures for the infinite periodic system on heating and cooling branches separately
Calculate finite-rod transmission spectra and map stop-band edges in temperature–frequency space
evidence_signal: The abstract reports calculated band structures and transmission spectra showing closed hysteresis loops and history-dependent transmission, with filling fraction also shifting gap positions.
watch_item: The effect is based on the thin-rod longitudinal-wave approximation and material-specific thermal paths, so real-device performance may be sensitive to fabrication tolerances, losses, and precise phase-fraction calibration.
paper_title: Hysteretic Acoustic Band Structures in Shape-Memory Composite Thin Rods
